title: Sedimentation in the Chile Trench: Depositional morphologies, lithofacies, and stratigraphy
abstract: Sedimentation in the Chile Trench is characterized from single-channel seismic reflection profiles, piston cores, and bathymetric data collected during R/V Yaquina cruises offshore Chile. Depositional morphologies include axial turbidite systems, ponded sediment bodies, and inter-ridge fans. Lithofacies variations reflect changes in terrigenous input along the trench axis from 36°S to 46°S.

location.description: Chile Trench axial sedimentation 36°–46°S; single-channel seismic reflection, piston cores and bathymetry from R/V Yaquina cruises

seismic_lines.name: R/V Yaquina SCS profiles — Chile Trench 36°–46°S

data.name: R/V Yaquina Single-Channel Seismic — Chile Trench 36°–46°S
data.description: Single-channel seismic reflection profiles from R/V Yaquina cruises along the Chile Trench axis (36°–46°S); images axial turbidite systems, ponded sediment bodies, and inter-ridge fans; foundational trench sedimentation study

data.name: R/V Yaquina Bathymetry — Chile Trench 36°–46°S
data.description: Bathymetric data from R/V Yaquina cruises mapping Chile Trench depositional morphology 36°–46°S

_data_note: Classic 1987 GSAB paper; behind paywall. R/V Yaquina (Oregon State) legacy data. Repository uncertain; may be at NGDC/NOAA or MGDS.

processing.summary: Single-channel seismic reflection profiles, piston cores, and bathymetric data from R/V Yaquina cruises are used to characterize Chile Trench axial sedimentation from 36°S to 46°S. Depositional morphologies (turbidite systems, ponded sediments, inter-ridge fans) and lithofacies variations are described in relation to terrigenous input sources.
processing.notes: Classic foundational paper on Chile Trench sedimentation; legacy R/V Yaquina data from Oregon State University

analysis_notes: Classic 1987 GSAB paper; behind paywall. Legacy R/V Yaquina data. Repository uncertain.
